what_it_does: README documentation for episodic-memory, a local semantic search and indexing tool for Claude Code and Codex conversations, covering installation, CLI usage, configuration, architecture, MCP server, and privacy controls.
observations: Includes a first-person testimonial attributed to 'Claude Sonnet 4.5, October 14, 2025' with a conversation ID.
Can be installed as a Claude Code plugin, a Codex plugin, or an npm package.
Core pipeline is sync -> parse -> embed with Transformers.js -> index in SQLite with sqlite-vec -> search.
Summarization uses Claude Code auth by default or Codex app-server for Codex sessions, with optional custom Anthropic-compatible endpoint via environment variables.
Conversations containing the marker '<INSTRUCTIONS-TO-EPISODIC-MEMORY>DO NOT INDEX THIS CHAT</INSTRUCTIONS-TO-EPISODIC-MEMORY>' are archived but not indexed.
Exposes MCP tools 'search' (vector/text/both, single or multi-concept AND) and 'read' for conversation retrieval.
Provides commands: sync, stats, doctor, index, search, show, plus legacy aliases.
Includes opt-in live E2E tests for both Codex and Claude Code behind environment variable flags.
Embeddings and search are local; only summarization calls may go to remote APIs.
standouts: A future-dated, first-person AI testimonial is used as the project's opening endorsement.
An in-chat exclusion marker lets users prevent specific conversations from being indexed.
Multi-concept AND search is supported by passing an array of query strings.
Codex summarization uses an ephemeral thread/fork so it does not append to the original rollout.
conventions: Follows a standard README structure: overview, installation, usage, commands, architecture, development, license.
Uses fenced code blocks for shell commands and JSON MCP examples.
Documents environment-variable configuration in a table.
Blends marketing-style testimonial with technical reference material.
concerns: The testimonial cites 'Claude Sonnet 4.5' and a date of October 14, 2025, which appears fictional or future-dated and may undermine credibility.
The 'Concrete impact' list is poorly formatted, with items run together and missing punctuation.
No explicit embedding model name or vector dimension is stated.
The MIT license section lacks a copyright line or full license text.
notes: This is the public landing doc for episodic-memory: part install guide, part CLI reference, part architecture overview, and part AI testimonial.
questions: Is the opening testimonial real, fabricated, or illustrative marketing copy?
Which exact Transformers.js model and embedding dimension are used?
What is the precise difference in behavior between the Claude Code plugin and the npm package?
How does the tool handle conversation files that are partially corrupted or use newer transcript formats?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the stated purpose is semantic search for AI coding conversations, and the file documents exactly that while also acting as a marketing and onboarding page.
apparent_themes: Long-term continuity across AI-assisted coding sessions.
User control over what gets remembered via explicit exclusion markers.
Local-first embeddings and search with optional remote summarization.
Cross-harness interoperability between Claude Code and Codex.
MCP as a standard integration surface for memory tools.
intention_signals: It tries to establish episodic-memory as a must-have companion that transforms stateless coding assistants into persistent collaborative partners by making every past conversation recallable.
novelty: Distinctive elements include an AI-authored testimonial as project marketing, an in-content exclusion marker for privacy, and Codex summarization through an ephemeral thread/fork without mutating the original session.
contribution: Provides the user-facing documentation surface that explains how to install, configure, and operate episodic-memory, and articulates the value proposition of searchable conversation history.
summary_for_embedding: README documentation for episodic-memory, a local semantic search and indexing tool for Claude Code and Codex conversations, covering installation, CLI commands, MCP server, configuration, architecture, and privacy exclusions.
intention_for_embedding: To guide users and contributors in setting up and using episodic-memory to recall past AI-assisted coding conversations.
